div.h2: Timer Cancel: Swap-With-Last Pattern and Dual Sift to Restore Heap Order

g.text: Frame 1: Initial Heap — Cancel Target at idx=2

div.p:
Step 1: Record cancelled_fd = fd[idx=2] = fd=5
Set conn_table[fd=5].timer_id = TIMER_ID_NONE (= -1)
last = heap_size - 1 = 5; idx(2) != last(5) → proceed with swap

Step 2: heap_swap(h, idx=2, last=5) performs:
• Swap timer_heap[2] ↔ timer_heap[5]
• Update timer_pool[slot].heap_idx for both
• Update conn_t->timer_id for both fds
Step 3: heap_size-- → 5 (idx=5 now out of range)

ol.li:
timer_pool[slot_11].heap_idx = 2 ✓
Dual Sift Logic: Only one direction ever performs swaps; the other is a no-op. Handles all swap-in locations.

div.p:
Parent check:
parent_idx = (2-1)/2 = 0 (1000ms)
node.expiry = 1800ms
1000 ≤ 1800 → NO SWAP

Children check:
Children 5,6 ≥ size 5
NO SWAP
